# Getting Started

This guide covers the local development and smoke-test workflow for Dockyard.

## Keep generated files outside the Dockyard repository

Generated packages, deployment values, env files, rendered output, and package archives should live outside the Dockyard source repository. This avoids accidentally committing operator-owned values or secrets.

Recommended layout from the Dockyard repository root:

```text
dockyard/              # Dockyard source repository
../dockyard-work/      # generated test packages
../deploy-values/      # operator values and env files
../dockyard-artifacts/ # generated package archives and rendered files
```

Create the folders:

```powershell
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force ..\dockyard-work, ..\deploy-values, ..\dockyard-artifacts | Out-Null
```

Linux/macOS:

```bash
mkdir -p ../dockyard-work ../deploy-values ../dockyard-artifacts
```

## Build

Using Make for first-time local development:

```bash
make dev-build
make test
```

For a non-mutating build, run `go mod tidy` explicitly first when needed:

```bash
go mod tidy
make build
```

`make build` uses `go env GOEXE`, so it writes `bin/dockyard.exe` on Windows and `bin/dockyard` on Linux/macOS. It does not update `go.mod` or `go.sum`.

Before committing:

```bash
make verify
```

Manual PowerShell build:

```powershell
go mod tidy
go test ./...
go build -o bin/dockyard.exe ./cmd/dockyard
```

Manual Linux/macOS build:

```bash
go mod tidy
go test ./...
go build -o bin/dockyard ./cmd/dockyard
```

## Windows smoke test

This smoke test was validated with Docker Desktop and PowerShell.

```powershell
.\bin\dockyard.exe doctor

.\bin\dockyard.exe init ..\dockyard-work\example-app

.\bin\dockyard.exe values template ..\dockyard-work\example-app `
  -o ..\deploy-values\local.yaml

.\bin\dockyard.exe lint ..\dockyard-work\example-app `
  -f ..\deploy-values\local.yaml

.\bin\dockyard.exe render ..\dockyard-work\example-app `
  -f ..\deploy-values\local.yaml `
  --validate-compose

.\bin\dockyard.exe install example ..\dockyard-work\example-app `
  -f ..\deploy-values\local.yaml

.\bin\dockyard.exe status example --compose-ps

Invoke-WebRequest http://localhost:8080 -UseBasicParsing

.\bin\dockyard.exe uninstall example
```

PowerShell aliases `curl` to `Invoke-WebRequest` in some environments. Use `Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing` or `curl.exe`.

## Starter package behavior

The package generated by `dockyard init` is intentionally runnable with the stock `nginx` image. It keeps `no-new-privileges`, but does not enable every hardening option by default because some images need writable runtime directories.

See [Security](security.md) for hardening guidance.

## Example packages

The repository includes a few package examples:

```text
examples/nginx
examples/postgres-app
examples/caddy-letsencrypt
examples/nginx-tls-mounted-certs
examples/traefik-letsencrypt
```

Use `examples/nginx` for quick local smoke tests. Use the Caddy, nginx TLS, and Traefik examples to learn different TLS packaging patterns.

Dockyard can render and validate these packages, but certificate issuance and reverse proxy behavior are handled by the underlying containers and Docker Compose.
